title: NOC 41311 – Probation and Parole Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Probation and Parole Officers under NOC 41311 are justice system professionals responsible for supervising offenders in the community, supporting rehabilitation, and promoting public safety. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role plays a critical function within Canada’s correctional and criminal justice systems. These officers work with courts, correctional institutions, and community agencies to help offenders reintegrate into society while ensuring compliance with legal conditions.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group413 – Professional occupations in social and community servicesMinor Group4131 – Police investigators and probation officersNOC Code41311

duties: Probation and Parole Officers perform some or all of the following duties:
Interview inmates to assess rehabilitation needs
Prepare pre-sentence and security assessment reports
Recommend security levels and intervention strategies
Interview offenders, family members, police, and employers
Supervise compliance with probation and parole conditions
Monitor offender progress and community adjustment
Refer offenders to social service and community programs
Initiate court action if probation or parole conditions are violated
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in:Social workCriminologyPsychologySociologyRelated social science discipline
Master’s degree may be required in:Social workCriminologyPsychology
Experience in correctional or social service settings
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment for immigration purposes.
salary_prospects: Probation and Parole Officers typically earn between:
$53,900 – $97,600 per year, depending on province, experience, and correctional level (provincial or federal).
Officers in Northern territories may receive additional allowances.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 41311 is stable due to:
Competition may vary depending on province and security requirements.
labour_market_demand: Probation and Parole Officers are employed by:
Opportunities are available across provinces, particularly in Ontario, Quebec, Alberta, British Columbia, and Northern territories.
Career advancement into supervisory or policy roles
Meaningful impact on community safety and rehabilitation
However, employment in correctional services often requires Canadian citizenship or permanent residency and security clearance.
pathways: Candidates must meet security and hiring requirements for correctional services.
